allies.rationale_text: Anchoring operates through System 1's associative activation — the first number or frame encountered shapes all subsequent estimates automatically without conscious intent.
allies.source_quote: Anchoring is dangerous because the initial piece of information causes one to put on blinders. This fixation can activate confirmation bias.

allies.rationale_text: Confirmation bias is partly System 1's pattern-completion drive — the system finishes stories it has started, treating incoming evidence as confirmation rather than new data to evaluate.
allies.source_quote: Confirmation bias is a prime example of a bias stemming from System 1 (reflexive, quick) thinking. System 1 automatically generates a coherent story from the evidence at hand.

antagonists.rationale_text: System 2 is the corrective counterpart — it applies effortful, rule-governed deliberation that can override System 1's fast outputs, but only when actively engaged and not depleted.
antagonists.source_quote: System 2 allocates attention to effortful mental activities and can override System 1 outputs when engaged.

antagonists.rationale_text: Probabilistic thinking requires computing distributions and base rates — precisely the operations System 1 cannot perform, defaulting instead to singular vivid cases over statistical regularities.
antagonists.source_quote: Probabilistic thinking requires computing distributions and base rates — precisely the operations System 1 cannot perform.

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical thinking demands questioning assumptions and suspending judgment — System 2 activities that System 1 actively undermines by delivering confident, fluent conclusions that do not signal the need for scrutiny.
antagonists.source_quote: Critical thinking demands questioning one's own biases and continually updating beliefs based on new data.

antagonists.rationale_text: System 1's case-based reasoning systematically ignores base rates, substituting vivid analogical reasoning — correct use of base rates requires explicitly overriding System 1 intuitions with reference-class data.
antagonists.source_quote: System 1 overweights vivid, specific case information and underweights dull but statistically authoritative base-rate data.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-1 conflicts with system-2 when fast associative pattern-matching bypasses the effortful deliberation that complex or novel problems require.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-1 vs system-2: system-1 conflicts with system-2 (System 2 slow thinking) when fast associative pattern-matching bypasses the effortful deliberation that complex or novel problems require.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-1 conflicts with probabilistic-thinking when intuitive case-based reasoning substitutes for distributional base-rate analysis.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-1 vs probabilistic-thinking: system-1 conflicts with probabilistic-thinking (probabilistic thinking) when intuitive case-based reasoning substitutes for distributional base-rate analysis.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-1 conflicts with base-rates when vivid case details activate System 1's pattern-completion drive, crowding out the statistical regularities that base-rate reasoning requires.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-1 vs base-rates: system-1 conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when vivid case details activate System 1's pattern-completion drive, crowding out the statistical regularities that base-rate reasoning requires.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-1 conflicts with critical-thinking when fluent, confident outputs suppress the signal that scrutiny, gap-identification, and assumption-testing are needed.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-1 vs critical-thinking: system-1 conflicts with critical-thinking (critical thinking) when fluent, confident outputs generated without deliberation suppress the signal that scrutiny is needed.

curation_notes.summary: System 1 Wave 3 curated from the new source markdown. Key allies: anchoring and confirmation-bias (both operate through System 1 mechanisms). Key antagonists: system-2 (corrective), probabilistic-thinking, critical-thinking, base-rates.
open_questions: Additional System 1 ally candidates: availability-heuristic, affect-heuristic — both are specific System 1 mechanisms. Deferred pending those canonical model ids.
